ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Nara tima maga funɛ kaali, o la zu be ti dɛ chɔɔli ti a kpak o nin. O funɛ ya chene, be ti vaan si o sou ni.
translated: If you don't know how to dance, you say that the drumming is not good. If you know how, you dance and everyone will watch you.

ref: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
text: Nikumɛ ta wa haasi kɔsi ta a suk din a du a ni. O zi wa du chene tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha.
translated: I am going to the market to buy a dress for my mother. She will be very happy when she sees it.

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: O fun a nyɛ zɛmɛ ta maga kani tɛɛnɛ ni.
translated: I am going to the market to buy some things.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un kpok ni, “Kila.

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: A wuzu ti nha funɛ wa chel, o fun ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Ma nyɛ ya la ji bɛɛŋ ti jibaŋ kinkin ti.”
translated: When the hare went to his in-law's house, he told the children that, "I have something that makes a jingling sound."
